text.title: Agent Postmortem Template (Agent-as-Defendant) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-postmortem-template enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every postmortem records the exact model identifier (provider plus version pin, e.g. anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) and deployment timestamp. 'Claude' or 'GPT-4' fails the template gate.
rule.rationale: Half of agent regressions are silent model upgrades. Without a pinned version string the postmortem cannot distinguish a prompt bug from a provider-side model swap.

rule.statement: Every postmortem includes a byte-exact prompt diff: system prompt at the time of incident vs system prompt at the last green eval run, with all variables substituted.
rule.rationale: Prompt regressions are the #1 cause of agent incidents and are invisible without a rendered byte-diff.

rule.statement: Layer Attribution assigns root cause to exactly one of six layers (model, system prompt, tool schema, tool result, retrieved context, user input). If the team cannot pick one, escalate to a second reviewer; do not close.
rule.rationale: 'Combination of factors' forecloses corrective action; forcing a single primary layer routes the fix to the owner who can act.

rule.statement: Eval delta is recorded: score on the regression case before the fix, target score after. Closure requires the after-fix eval to be green.
rule.rationale: Without eval delta the fix is theatre; eval-gated closure prevents same hallucination class re-shipping.

rule.statement: Replay artefacts (rendered prompt, tool-call payloads, tool results, retrieved chunks, response) are attached as files or trace links. If a layer's artefact cannot be replayed, the postmortem records a Trace Gap with an explicit instrumentation action item.
rule.rationale: Replay is the difference between a postmortem and a story.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
